title: Routes Cheat Sheet
description: A concise guide to managing routes in web applications and APIs.

commands.description: Defines a GET route for retrieving all users.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to define a route to fetch all user data from the server.

commands.description: Mounts the API routes under the '/api' path.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to group API routes under a common path prefix.

commands.description: Defines a GET route in Laravel for listing users.
commands.scenario: Use when setting up a route in a Laravel application to list users.

commands.description: Defines a POST route for user login.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to handle user login via a POST request.

commands.description: Creates RESTful routes for users in a Rails application.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to quickly generate all RESTful routes for a resource in Rails.

commands.description: Defines a PUT route for updating a user's information.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to update user details via an HTTP PUT request.

commands.description: Defines a DELETE route in Laravel for removing a user.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to delete a user record in a Laravel application.

commands.description: Chains GET and POST routes for products on the same path.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to define multiple HTTP methods on the same route path.

commands.description: Creates a resource route for photos in Laravel.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to set up a full set of RESTful routes for a resource in Laravel.

commands.description: Defines a simple GET route that returns a status message.
commands.scenario: Use when you need a basic health check endpoint in your application.

commands.description: Defines a PATCH route for partially updating a user's information.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to apply partial updates to a user's data.

commands.description: Defines a route in Laravel that responds to both GET and POST requests.
commands.scenario: Use when you need a route to handle multiple HTTP methods in Laravel.

commands.description: Defines a DELETE route for removing a post.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to delete a post resource via an HTTP DELETE request.
